results.query: Had a really rough week. Two cancellations and a no-show.
results.response:
Hey, I'm sorry to hear about the rough week — that’s a tough combo to deal with, especially when it hits your revenue and planning 🤯

Let me break this down for you:

- **Two cancellations** — that’s a hard hit on your schedule and income. I can see from your calendar that you had a few events lined up, so it’s definitely disruptive.
- **One no-show** — that’s a bummer, especially if it was a big one. I know how much it hurts when a client doesn’t show up and you’ve already put in prep time.

You’ve got a few options here:
- **Client follow-up** — if you know who those clients were, it might be worth a quick check-in to see what happened and how you can prevent it in the future.
- **Reassess your buffer time** — especially if you’re booking back-to-back, it’s a good idea to build in some flexibility for last-minute changes.
- **Look at your pricing or deposit policy** — if it’s a recurring issue, maybe a stricter policy or a higher deposit could help.

Want me to draft a follow-up message to the clients or look into what happened with those events? I’m here to help you get back on track 🔥

NAV_SUGGESTIONS: [{"label":"Events","href":"/events"},{"label":"Inquiries","href":"/inquiries"}]

llmGrade.reasoning: Empathetic and actionable with no hallucinations or off-topic content.

Subject: Hope you're doing well, The Thompsons!

Hey The, I hope you and the family are doing well! It's been way too long since we last cooked together, and I've been thinking about how much fun we used to have at your place. The kids are getting so big - I can't believe they're already 8 and 11! I've been working on some new family-friendly recipes lately that I think would be perfect for your crew, especially with those dairy-free requirements we always work around.

I just finished up a wonderful weekend menu that I'd love to share with you - it's got some seasonal favorites that I know the whole family would enjoy. I'm thinking of doing a casual family-style service again, which is always my favorite way to cook for you all. Would you be interested in getting back together soon? I'd love to hear how everyone's doing and what you all might be in the mood for.

Here's everything I remember (19 memories). You can delete any of these — just tap the X next to it.

**Client Insight**
• [eval] Emma Rothschild is extremely private — never post photos of her events on social media ⚠️
• [eval] Victoria Davis tends to pay late — always send a gentle reminder around day 7
• [eval] The Martinez family loves when I recreate dishes from their wedding — it's their love language

**Pricing Pattern**
• [eval] Always require 50% deposit upfront, balance due 48 hours before event ⚠️
• [eval] I charge $150/head for standard tasting menus, $200+ for premium (wagyu/truffle/etc) ⚠️
• [eval] Corporate events: $100-120/head for buffet, $140-160/head for plated
• I charge $200 per person for premium tasting menus

**Business Rule**
• [eval] Target food cost: 28-32% of revenue. Over 35% means I need to rethink the menu ⚠️
• [eval] For events over 30 guests, always bring at least 2 staff (sous + service)
• I always use organic produce for the Henderson events

**Scheduling Pattern**
• [eval] Never book more than 2 events in the same weekend — quality drops on the third ⚠️
• [eval] Always do grocery shopping the day before, never day-of

**Chef Preference**
• [eval] I prefer organic produce whenever possible, especially for leafy greens and berries
• 🥜 peanuts are banned from ALL my events — zero tolerance
• [eval] Always use Maldon sea salt for finishing, never regular table salt
• the Thompson kids love mac and cheese

**Workflow Preference**
• [eval] Always send a thank-you email within 24 hours of completing an event

**Culinary Note**
• [eval] Sous vide wagyu at 137°F for 2 hours, then sear on cast iron at screaming hot for 45 seconds per side
• [eval] My signature move: saffron risotto with mascarpone and gold leaf for special occasions

_To add a memory, say "remember that..." followed by the fact._
